methodology.approach: Instruction-level machine analysis with source-scope aggregation inspired by LLVM IR energy characterization and scope propagation.
attribution: Assign per-instruction energy from an explicit model.
Aggregate raw energy at block and function scope.
Apply block-frequency weighting for hotspot ranking.
Propagate debug locations into source-line energy scopes.
limitations: This model is comparative and static, not a calibrated physical measurement for modern desktop x86 microarchitectures.
Unmapped opcodes fall back to bucket heuristics or the default fallback cost.

notes: The model is meant to support comparative hotspot analysis and source attribution.
Alias coverage should be expanded from real opcode traces collected from representative workloads.
The pass emits structured function, block, and line scopes so downstream consumers can evolve without changing the LLVM output format.
